title: podman chdir to current working directory: Permission denied
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman chdir to the current working directory then segfault when running `podman secret ls`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -o nounset
set -o errexit
set -o xtrace

TESTUSER=testuser
DIR="/tmp/bugdemo"

rm -rf "$DIR"
mkdir -p "$DIR"
cd "$DIR"
chmod -R 700 .
ls -al
if id "$TESTUSER"; then
  sudo userdel --remove "$TESTUSER"
fi
sudo useradd --create-home "$TESTUSER"
sudo --user "$TESTUSER" -- podman secret ls
sudo --user "testuser" -- podman ps
# Run the podman command from the TESTUSER shell
# including loading the shell configuration
sudo --login --user "$TESTUSER" -- podman secret ls
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```bash
% ./bug.sh 
+ TESTUSER=testuser
+ DIR=/tmp/bugdemo
+ rm -rf /tmp/bugdemo
+ mkdir -p /tmp/bugdemo
+ cd /tmp/bugdemo
+ chmod -R 700 .
+ ls -al
total 0
drwx------  2 user user   40 May 27 15:26 .
drwxrwxrwt 49 root   root   1240 May 27 15:26 ..
+ id testuser
uid=1003(testuser) gid=1004(testuser) groups=1004(testuser)
+ sudo userdel --remove testuser
userdel: testuser mail spool (/var/spool/mail/testuser) not found
+ sudo useradd --create-home testuser
+ sudo --user testuser -- podman secret ls
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1003` (possibly as root)

cannot chdir to /tmp/bugdemo: Permission denied
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x108 pc=0x563f2e0d8536]

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod.(*Runtime).GetSecretsStorageDir(0xc0002a36c0, 0x11, 0x8f0000c00005ec00)
        libpod/runtime.go:911 +0x36
github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/domain/infra/abi.(*ContainerEngine).SecretList(0xc0000103a8, 0x563f2ea886f8, 0xc0000440c0, 0xc000531d30, 0x563f2d66d5ca, 0xc0000c9200, 0xc000531d08, 0x12)
        pkg/domain/infra/abi/secrets.go:74 +0x48
github.com/containers/podman/v3/cmd/podman/secrets.ls(0x563f2f475860, 0x563f2f539568, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0)
        cmd/podman/secrets/list.go:54 +0x64
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).execute(0x563f2f475860, 0xc00003c1a0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x563f2f475860, 0xc00003c1a0)
        vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:852 +0x472
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteC(0x563f2f47aae0, 0xc0000440c0, 0x563f2e80d100, 0x563f2f539568)
        vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:960 +0x375
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).Execute(...)
        vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:897
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteContext(...)
        vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:890
main.Execute()
        cmd/podman/root.go:90 +0xe9
main.main()
        cmd/podman/main.go:37 +0x94
```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

laptop and google cloud

google cloud getting the following error using podman 3.2.0:
```
Version:      3.2.0-dev

user@instance:~$ ./bug.sh 
+ TESTUSER=testuser
+ DIR=/tmp/bugdemo
+ rm -rf /tmp/bugdemo
+ mkdir -p /tmp/bugdemo
+ cd /tmp/bugdemo
+ chmod -R 700 .
+ ls -al
total 8
drwx------  2 user user 4096 May 27 22:32 .
drwxrwxrwt 20 root         root         4096 May 27 22:32 ..
+ id testuser
id: ‘testuser’: no such user
+ sudo useradd --create-home testuser
+ sudo --user testuser -- podman secret ls
cannot chdir to /tmp/bugdemo: Permission denied
Error: chown /home/testuser/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l: operation not permitted
```

comments.body:
use the latest code:
```
[root@Centos8 ~]# ./test.sh 
++ TESTUSER=testuser
++ DIR=/tmp/bugdemo
++ rm -rf /tmp/bugdemo
++ mkdir -p /tmp/bugdemo
++ cd /tmp/bugdemo
++ chmod -R 700 .
++ ls -al
total 0
drwx------  2 root root   6 May 28 09:39 .
drwxrwxrwt 11 root root 177 May 28 09:39 ..
++ id testuser
uid=1000(testuser) gid=1000(testuser) groups=1000(testuser)
++ sudo userdel --remove testuser
++ sudo useradd --create-home testuser
++ sudo --user testuser -- podman secret ls
cannot chdir to /tmp/bugdemo: Permission denied
Error: chown /home/testuser/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l: operation not permitted
```
I removed the segfault from the title if that part is solved in latest.
It looks like podman secret ls is trying to chdir to CWD, and since you set this up with permissions the testuser is not able to access, it is failing.
If you chown 755 /tmp/bugdemo, does it work?
@rhatdan yes, it would certainly work but everyone would have access to `/tmp/bugdemo`. Maybe podman should not chdir to CWD?
That is the default behaviour, Why not change the CWD so it is not /tmp/bugdemo?
As a workaround, I chdir to /tmp before calling podman. If accessing the CWD is useful to podman, then that's not a bug and I am happy to close that ticket.
You could also just chown the directory to 711 and then all tools could search to the Working directory, but would not be able to list or read any of the content in the directory.
